exercises.name: Push-Up
exercises.description: A classic upper body compound movement targeting the chest, shoulders, and triceps while engaging the core.
tips: Keep body in a straight line from head to heels
Don't let hips sag or pike

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.description: The king of lower-body exercises. Builds strength in quads, glutes, and hamstrings.
tips: Feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
Keep chest up and back straight
Descend until thighs are parallel to floor

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: An isometric core exercise that builds total body stability and endurance.
tips: Keep body in a straight line

exercises.name: Lunge
exercises.description: Unilateral lower body exercise that improves balance, coordination, and single-leg strength.
tips: Step forward far enough so front knee stays above ankle
Lower back knee toward the floor
Push through front heel to return

exercises.name: Downward Dog
exercises.description: A foundational yoga pose that lengthens the spine and back of the legs while building shoulder strength.
tips: Press firmly through palms and spread fingers
Try to straighten legs without rounding back

exercises.name: Warrior I
exercises.description: A standing yoga pose that builds strength, stability, and focus while opening the chest and hip flexors.
tips: Arms stretched overhead with palms facing each other

exercises.name: Warrior II
exercises.description: An empowering standing yoga pose that opens the hips and builds leg and core strength.

exercises.name: Tree Pose
exercises.description: A classic balance pose that improves focus, coordination, and single-leg stability.
tips: Press foot into inner thigh (not knee)
Grow tall through the crown of your head

exercises.name: Child's Pose
exercises.description: A restorative resting pose that gently stretches the lower back and opens the hips.
tips: Extend arms forward and relax forehead on mat

exercises.name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
exercises.description: A gentle standing stretch targeting the back of the thighs and lower back.
tips: Bend from the hips, not the waist
Soft bend in knees if tight

exercises.name: Shoulder Opener
exercises.description: A gentle shoulder mobility exercise to open the chest and relieve upper body tension.
tips: Don't force the range of motion

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description: Essential stretch for anyone who sits for long periods. Opens the hip flexors and relieves lower back tension.
tips: Keep back knee on soft surface
